title: Richmond Bridge works begin to preserve convict-era landmark
australian_relation: Exact ABC public page naming Richmond Bridge as the setting of a source-stated Tasmanian ghost legend.
coordinate_evidence_note: Public page text names `Richmond Bridge` in a ghost/supernatural context; gazetteer result state-checked as Richmond Bridge, Wellington Street, Hobart, Tasmania, 7025, Australia.

(ABC News: Rhiannon Shine) abc.net.au/news/richmond-bridge-conservation-works-tasmania/8347030 Link copied ShareShare article Australia's oldest bridge, which many believe is haunted by a notorious ex-convict, is getting some TLC to help it last into the future. The Richmond Bridge, which spans the Coal River in Tasmania's south, was built by convict labour in 1823 and completed two.
